methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Deepnight is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $5M+. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public and 8 private—spanning the same sector and development stage, including Series A peers Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M), alongside Seed-stage Tenna Systems ($60M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $5M raised as a proxy valuation and applying a typical 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage multiplier, implies a valuation of $12M. Peer-median methodology examined the four sampled comparables, which span $60M to $500M, yielding a median of $225M and implying a valuation at that median. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in financing history and de-risking across the peer set—particularly the gap between the Seed-stage Tenna Systems and Series A companies.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $11M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $119M. The disparity between stage-progression ($12M) and peer-median ($225M) reflects the tension between Deepnight's current funding level and the valuations achieved by more advanced-stage peers in the same sector. Key uncertainties include Deepnight's technical differentiation relative to peers, capital efficiency to date, and whether near-term milestones justify Series A-equivalent valuation multiples.
